free_text: Cross sections and production yields of 117mSn and
other radionuclides generated in natural and enriched
antimony with protons up to 145 MeV
For initial proton energies about 159,
113 and 94 MeV
For proton energies 73 and 38 MeV
Every Sb-target was placed together with aluminum
(100+ -4 mu-m thickness) or/and copper (40+ -2 mu-m)
foils of the same form for beam intensity monitoring
and wrapped in aluminum foil envelopes.
The error in proton energy comprised
uncertainty of initial accelerator energy, target
thickness, proton straggling and scattering in the
targets, degraders and water or air gaps.
Errors in cross section values include
uncertainty in proton flux determination, detector
efficiency, statistics in gamma-ray spectra, and
geometry in gamma-ray measurement.
Entry was approved by Dr. S.V. Ermolaev
Initial proton energies were 158.8 +- 0.3, 94.4 +- 0.3,
72.7 +- 0.7, and 37.8 +- 0.4 MeV; beam currents (mu-A)
and irradiation times (h) were 1.2, 0.5, 0.07,
0.03 and 5.2, 9.8, 7.9, 8.2, correspondingly.
Thin metallic antimony foils of natural isotopic
composition (121Sb 57.21 %, 123Sb 42.79 %, purity
more or equal to 95 %) were used. Foils with
dimensions of 17 * 50 mm and nominal Sb thickness of
40 mu-m on 130 mu-m polyester backing were prepared
for irradiation with protons of initial energy 159 MeV
and 94 MeV; and 25 mu-m foils (170 mu-m of
polyester backing) with dimensions of 17 * 17 mm for
irradiation with normal to target surface beam of 73
MeV and 38 MeV protons.
Tbl. 4 from Radiochim. Acta, 108,327,2020

Tbl. 4 from Radiochim. Acta, 108,327,2020
Initial proton energies were 113.3 +- 0.3, 72.7 +- 0.7
MeV (as well as calculated from experiments with
natSb and 121Sb); beam currents (mu-A) and
irradiation times (h) were 0.5, 0.02 and 9.9, 5.3,
correspondingly.
Enriched material was in the
form of dispersed metallic grains. The material was
pounded in a mortar to fine powder and then it was
utilized for preparation of target with thickness
27-49 mg/cm by different methods.

2.5 % via 118gSb was detected.
Initial proton energies were 113.3 +- 0.3, 72.7 +- 0.7
MeV (as well as calculated from experiments with
natSb and 121Sb); beam currents (mu-A) and
irradiation times (h) were 0.5, 0.02 and 9.9, 5.3,
correspondingly.
Enriched material was in the
form of dispersed metallic grains. The material was
pounded in a mortar to fine powder and then it was
utilized for preparation of target with thickness
27-49 mg/cm by different methods.
